scope: Narrow the two wildcard entries `("*", "get")` and `("*", "post")` at crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs:2476-2477 (RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest member_patterns) to receiver-NAME-allowlist entries `("client", "get")`, `("client", "post")`, `("agent", "get")`, `("agent", "post")`, `("http", "get")`, `("http", "post")`, `("request_builder", "get")`, `("request_builder", "post")`, `("req", "get")`, `("req", "post")`. Per planning Option A. Eliminates 100% of synthetic FPs (HashMap/Vec/BTreeMap.get on tainted) measured at rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 M3 binary smoke (3/3 FP-on-get-callers → 0/3 expected post-M2). Preserves the load-bearing real-world `let client = reqwest::Client::new(); client.get(&url)` member-access idiom. Closes ZERO RED tests; this is a PRECISION milestone.
closes_carry_forward: rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 premortem dab0766 R8 (T2/E1 wildcard-get FP elephant) — quantified at M3 binary-smoke; this milestone narrows the wildcard per the carry_forward_recommendation.
enables_followons: (optional, out of this scope) rust-wildcard-receiver-type-aware-v1 — Option C type-walk receiver-type filtering for the residual gaps (`c.get`, `self.client.get`)
validator_verdict: PASS_AUTONOMOUS_READY (no premortem required; non-RED-closing precision narrowing)

validator_mandates.no_regression_on_4_rust_red_tests: rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 closed 4 Rust RED positives (rust_command_injection_positive, rust_path_traversal_positive, rust_ssrf_positive, rust_deserialization_positive). All 4 must REMAIN GREEN at every milestone gate. The 4 string-literal-FP regression-guards (rust_*_string_literal_fp) must STAY GREEN. Verified by reading the fixtures: rust_ssrf_positive uses `reqwest::blocking::get(&u)` (scoped-identifier — matched via `("", "reqwest::blocking::get")` raw-fallback, NOT via wildcard). The 3 other RED-closures don't use HttpRequest sinks at all. Therefore wildcard narrowing has ZERO impact on the 4 currently-GREEN tests.
validator_mandates.no_public_api_change: AstSinkPattern struct shape unchanged. VulnFinding shape unchanged. tldr_core::security::vuln::scan_vulnerabilities signature unchanged. The change is purely a static-array entry adjustment.
validator_mandates.no_new_enum_variants: VulnType, TaintSinkType, TaintSourceType — no new variants. The narrowing operates entirely within RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest entry's member_patterns array.
validator_mandates.additive_OR_narrowing_only_no_loosening: The 2 removed wildcard entries `("*", "get")` / `("*", "post")` are NARROWED to a finite allowlist of 5 receiver names (10 entries total: 5 names × 2 fields). The post-M2 set is a STRICT SUBSET of the pre-M2 wildcard's match universe. No NEW receiver/field combinations are added that weren't already covered by the wildcard. NO LOOSENING.
validator_mandates.atomic_narrowing_commit: M2 ships the 10-entry allowlist replacement in a SINGLE commit. The 2-line removal of `("*", "get")` and `("*", "post")` ships ATOMIC with the 10-line addition of the allowlist entries — there must NOT exist an intermediate SHA where the wildcard is removed but the allowlist is missing (would regress `let client = reqwest::Client::new(); client.get(&url)` real-world coverage between commits).
validator_mandates.synthetic_fp_quantification_pre_post: M1 captures pre-M2 FP counts on 3 synthetic FP fixtures (HashMap::get / Vec::get / BTreeMap::get on tainted) AND captures pre-M2 TP/positive counts on 3 synthetic TP fixtures (client.get / agent.get / reqwest::blocking::get on tainted). M2 verifies post-M2 0 FP findings on the 3 FP synthetics + ≥1 finding on each of `client.get` and `agent.get` (the load-bearing real-world idioms) + ≥1 finding on `reqwest::blocking::get` (the scoped-identifier path; unchanged). Quantifies before/after FP rate explicitly in M2-report.json.
validator_mandates.fixtures_not_added_to_test_corpus: The synthetic FP/TP fixtures used for M1/M2 quantification are CONSTRUCTED ad-hoc and run via the binary; NOT added to crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/ NOR to vuln_migration_v1_red.rs. Adds zero LOC to the test suite. Documented verbatim in M1-baseline-fp.json and M2-report.json.
validator_mandates.e2e_test_preservation_mandatory: All 36 test_e2e_* tests at crates/tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs:1568-2100 must REMAIN GREEN. Specifically test_e2e_rust_ssrf_reqwest_get at vuln.rs:1184-1193 uses `reqwest::get(target)` (scoped-identifier) — independent of wildcard.
validator_mandates.test_analyze_rust_unit_tests_preserved: All 9 #[test] fns in commands::remaining::vuln::tests module at crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:925-1037 must REMAIN GREEN. They call analyze_rust_file directly; do not exercise canonical taint pipeline.
validator_mandates.staging_method_explicit: Each commit stages exactly the listed files via `git add <path>...`. NO `git add -A` / `git add .`. Cargo.lock NEVER staged (`git checkout HEAD -- Cargo.lock` before each commit if dirty). Pre-existing dirty modifications to UNRELATED continuum/autonomous/* artifacts visible at planning-time `git status` are NOT staged by this milestone.
validator_mandates.no_push_no_publish_no_version_bump: All milestones internal. Local tag `rust-wildcard-get-narrowing-v1` only at M3. NO `cargo publish`, NO `git push`, NO version bump in Cargo.toml. USER STANDING RULE.

staging_method.verification: git show HEAD --stat | awk '/continuum\/autonomous\// { print }' | grep -v 'rust-wildcard-get-narrowing-v1-plan/' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK
staging_method.verification_note: Mirrors rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 staging convention. Manual `git show HEAD --stat` audit is canonical. Cargo.lock NEVER staged.
staging_method.rationale: Prevents accidental staging of unrelated continuum/autonomous artifacts (especially the existing unstaged modifications to vuln-migration-v1-plan/, regex-removal-v1-plan/, etc. visible at planning-time `git status`).

milestones.title: RED-confirm: 4 Rust positives still GREEN at HEAD + synthetic FP-baseline capture
milestones.scope: Run `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --test vuln_migration_v1_red rust_` at HEAD; capture all 4 positives GREEN + 4 string-literal-FP GREEN. Run `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --lib commands::remaining::vuln::tests` capturing 9/9 GREEN. Run all 36 test_e2e_* tests in tldr-core. Build release binary with --features semantic. Construct 6 synthetic Rust fixtures and run `tldr vuln <fixture> --lang rust --format json` on each; record finding counts verbatim. The 6 fixtures are: (i) HashMap::get on tainted key (FP), (ii) Vec::get on tainted index (FP), (iii) BTreeMap::get on tainted key (FP), (iv) `let client = reqwest::Client::new(); client.get(&url)` on tainted url (TP — real-world idiom MUST be preserved post-M2), (v) `let agent = ureq::agent(); agent.get(&url)` on tainted url (TP — real-world idiom MUST be preserved post-M2), (vi) `reqwest::blocking::get(&url)` on tainted url (TP — scoped-identifier path; unchanged by narrowing). Pre-M2 expectation: (i)/(ii)/(iii) all emit ≥1 Ssrf finding (FPs from wildcard); (iv)/(v) emit ≥1 Ssrf finding (TPs from wildcard); (vi) emits ≥1 Ssrf finding (TP from scoped-identifier raw-fallback).
milestones.loc_delta_estimate: 0 LOC source code; ~150 LOC across 3 report artifacts
stop_thresholds: All 4 currently-GREEN Rust positives confirmed GREEN at HEAD: rust_command_injection_positive, rust_deserialization_positive, rust_path_traversal_positive, rust_ssrf_positive (closed by rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 M2).
All 4 rust_*_string_literal_fp regression-guards confirmed GREEN at HEAD.
9/9 #[test] fns in commands::remaining::vuln::tests module confirmed GREEN at HEAD.
All 36 test_e2e_* tests in tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs confirmed GREEN at HEAD.
vuln_migration_v1_composite_red 1/1 passing confirmed GREEN at HEAD.
Synthetic FP fixture (i) HashMap::get: ≥1 Ssrf finding emitted (pre-M2 baseline).
Synthetic FP fixture (ii) Vec::get: ≥1 Ssrf finding emitted (pre-M2 baseline).
Synthetic FP fixture (iii) BTreeMap::get: ≥1 Ssrf finding emitted (pre-M2 baseline).
Synthetic TP fixture (iv) client.get member-access: ≥1 Ssrf finding emitted (pre-M2 baseline; load-bearing).
Synthetic TP fixture (v) agent.get member-access: ≥1 Ssrf finding emitted (pre-M2 baseline; load-bearing).
Synthetic TP fixture (vi) reqwest::blocking::get scoped-identifier: ≥1 Ssrf finding emitted (pre-M2 baseline; unchanged-path).

milestones.title: ATOMIC narrow wildcards + verify
milestones.scope: In crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs at the RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest entry (lines 2473-2488): replace the two wildcard entries `("*", "get")` and `("*", "post")` (currently at lines 2476-2477) with the 10-entry receiver-NAME allowlist: `("client", "get")`, `("client", "post")`, `("agent", "get")`, `("agent", "post")`, `("http", "get")`, `("http", "post")`, `("request_builder", "get")`, `("request_builder", "post")`, `("req", "get")`, `("req", "post")`. Update the doc-comment at lines 2459-2472 to retire the wildcard reference and document the allowlist convention. ATOMIC: the 2-line removal + 10-line addition + doc-comment update ship in ONE commit. Re-run all 6 synthetic fixtures from M1; record post-M2 counts; verify (i)/(ii)/(iii) drop to 0 Ssrf findings, (iv)/(v) STAY ≥1 Ssrf finding (load-bearing real-world idioms preserved), (vi) STAYS ≥1 Ssrf finding (scoped-identifier unchanged).

ATOMIC: single commit. Wildcard removal + allowlist addition + doc-comment update ship together with NO intermediate commit.

rollback_plan.if_M2_breaks_currently_green_test: git revert <M2_sha>. The atomic commit means revert is single-step. Wildcards return; FP rate returns to 100% on synthetic .get callers but no GREEN test regresses. Triage which receiver-name allowlist entry was insufficient (most likely a real-world fixture uses a receiver name like `c` / `r` / `self.client` not in the allowlist) and surface as a follow-on or expand the allowlist incrementally.
rollback_plan.if_M2_load_bearing_real_world_breaks: If post-M2 a load-bearing real-world idiom regresses (e.g., a downstream user reports `let c = reqwest::Client::new(); c.get(...)` no longer detected), the immediate response is to add the missing receiver name to the allowlist in a follow-on M4 (additive). Do NOT re-introduce the wildcard.
rollback_plan.if_M3_surfaces_unanticipated_concern: Document in M3-report.json with non-additive-resolution rationale; do NOT extend milestone scope. The narrowing is the binary contract for this milestone.

success_summary_post_m3.synthetic_fp_rate_on_get_callers: Pre-M2: 100% (3/3 FP). Post-M2: 0% (0/3 FP). Net +100 percentage-point precision improvement on the .get(<tainted>) member-access FP class quantified by rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 M3 binary smoke.
success_summary_post_m3.load_bearing_real_world_idioms_preserved: client.get / agent.get / http.get / req.get / request_builder.get all retain Ssrf detection via the new allowlist entries.
success_summary_post_m3.documented_residual_gaps: Receiver names outside the allowlist (`c`, `r`, `self.client`, etc.) no longer trigger Ssrf detection on member-access shape. Documented as known carry-forward for future Option C type-aware filtering work.
success_summary_post_m3.carry_forward_aggregate_post_m3: rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 M5 retained-RED set unchanged (still 4 retained: 3 nested-constructor + 1 Ruby backtick if those haven't already closed). This milestone does NOT change the carry-forward count; it only improves precision.

post_publish_unblock: After M3 lands, the v0.2.x release group's `tldr vuln` produces lower FP rates on real-world Rust codebases — significant UX improvement. CHANGELOG flags the precision change so consumers understand why their .rs file finding counts may decrease.
